questions.question: Which row describes what happens to the particles in solid iodine when it is heated and turned into a gas? separation of particles speed of particles A closer together faster, B closer together slower, C further apart faster, D further apart slower
questions.text: Which row describes what happens to the particles in solid iodine when it is heated and turned into a gas? separation of particles speed of particles A closer together faster, B closer together slower, C further apart faster, D further apart slower

questions.question: A student put exactly 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid into a conical flask. The student added 2.5 g of solid sodium carbonate and measured the change in temperature of the mixture. Which apparatus does the student need to use? A balance, measuring cylinder, thermometer, B balance, pipette, stopwatch, C balance, pipette, thermometer, D burette, pipette, thermometer
questions.text: A student put exactly 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid into a conical flask. The student added 2.5 g of solid sodium carbonate and measured the change in temperature of the mixture. Which apparatus does the student need to use? A balance, measuring cylinder, thermometer, B balance, pipette, stopwatch, C balance, pipette, thermometer, D burette, pipette, thermometer

questions.question: A student separates sugar from pieces of broken glass by dissolving the sugar in water and filtering off the broken glass. filtrate What is the filtrate? A broken glass only, B broken glass and sugar solution, C pure water, D sugar solution 3
questions.text: A student separates sugar from pieces of broken glass by dissolving the sugar in water and filtering off the broken glass. filtrate What is the filtrate? A broken glass only, B broken glass and sugar solution, C pure water, D sugar solution 3
options: B broken glass and sugar solution

questions.question: How many protons, neutrons and electrons are there in one atom of the isotope 27 13 Al? protons    neutrons   electrons A 13 13 13, B 13 14 13, C 14 13 13, D 14 14 13
questions.text: How many protons, neutrons and electrons are there in one atom of the isotope 27 13 Al? protons    neutrons   electrons A 13 13 13, B 13 14 13, C 14 13 13, D 14 14 13

questions.question: Which description of brass is correct? A alloy, B compound, C element, D non-metal
questions.text: Which description of brass is correct? A alloy, B compound, C element, D non-metal

questions.question: Rubidium is in Group I and iodine is in Group VII of the Periodic Table. Which row describes what happens when rubidium and iodine react together to form rubidium iodide? rubidium iodine A each atom gains one electron each atom loses one electron, B each atom loses one electron each atom gains one electron, C each atom loses more than one electron   each atom gains more than one electron D each atom neither gains nor loses an electron each atom neither gains nor loses an electron
questions.text: Rubidium is in Group I and iodine is in Group VII of the Periodic Table. Which row describes what happens when rubidium and iodine react together to form rubidium iodide? rubidium iodine A each atom gains one electron each atom loses one electron, B each atom loses one electron each atom gains one electron, C each atom loses more than one electron   each atom gains more than one electron D each atom neither gains nor loses an electron each atom neither gains nor loses an electron
options: A each atom gains one electron each atom loses one electron
B each atom loses one electron each atom gains one electron
C each atom loses more than one electron   each atom gains more than one electron D each atom neither gains nor loses an electron each atom neither gains nor loses an electron

questions.text: Which row shows the properties for an ionic compound? volatility electrical conductivity when solid A high good, B high poor, C low good, D low poor 4

questions.question: Iron can be electroplated with zinc to make it resistant to corrosion. Which row about electroplating iron with zinc is correct? positive electrode (anode) negative electrode (cathode) electrolyte A iron zinc iron nitrate, B iron zinc zinc nitrate, C zinc iron iron nitrate, D zinc iron zinc nitrate
questions.text: Iron can be electroplated with zinc to make it resistant to corrosion. Which row about electroplating iron with zinc is correct? positive electrode (anode) negative electrode (cathode) electrolyte A iron zinc iron nitrate, B iron zinc zinc nitrate, C zinc iron iron nitrate, D zinc iron zinc nitrate

questions.question: An  energy  level  diagram  for  the  reaction  between  substance  X  and  substance  Y  to  form substance Z is shown. X + Y Z energy progress of reaction Which statement is correct? A Energy is released as substance Z is formed., B Substance Z has more energy than substance X and substance Y., C The reaction is exothermic., D When substance X and substance Y react, the temperature increases. 5
questions.text: An  energy  level  diagram  for  the  reaction  between  substance  X  and  substance  Y  to  form substance Z is shown. X + Y Z energy progress of reaction Which statement is correct? A Energy is released as substance Z is formed., B Substance Z has more energy than substance X and substance Y., C The reaction is exothermic., D When substance X and substance Y react, the temperature increases. 5
options: A Energy is released as substance Z is formed.
B Substance Z has more energy than substance X and substance Y.
D When substance X and substance Y react, the temperature increases. 5

questions.question: Which reactions are exothermic? 1   C  +  O 2   CO 2 2   CH 4 +  2O 2   CO 2 +  2H 2 O 3   2H 2 +  O 2   2H 2 O A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C  1 and 3 only D  2 and 3 only
questions.text: Which reactions are exothermic? 1   C  +  O 2   CO 2 2   CH 4 +  2O 2   CO 2 +  2H 2 O 3   2H 2 +  O 2   2H 2 O A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C  1 and 3 only D  2 and 3 only
options: A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C  1 and 3 only D  2 and 3 only

questions.question: Solid copper( II) carbonate reacts with dilute sulfuric acid. CuCO 3 +  H 2 SO 4   CuSO 4 +  CO 2 +  H 2 O The rate of the reaction can be changed by varying the conditions. Which changes always increase the rate of this chemical reaction? 1   increasing the concentration of sulfuric acid 2   increasing the size of the pieces of copper( II) carbonate 3   increasing the temperature 4   increasing the volume of sulfuric acid A 1, 3 and 4 B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4 only
questions.text: Solid copper( II) carbonate reacts with dilute sulfuric acid. CuCO 3 +  H 2 SO 4   CuSO 4 +  CO 2 +  H 2 O The rate of the reaction can be changed by varying the conditions. Which changes always increase the rate of this chemical reaction? 1   increasing the concentration of sulfuric acid 2   increasing the size of the pieces of copper( II) carbonate 3   increasing the temperature 4   increasing the volume of sulfuric acid A 1, 3 and 4 B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4 only
options: A 1, 3 and 4 B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4 only

questions.question: When a piece of marble is added to hydrochloric acid, bubbles of carbon dioxide gas are given off. Which method is used to find the rate of the reaction? A counting the number of gas bubbles formed, B measuring the diameter of the gas bubbles, C measuring the speed at which the gas bubbles rise upwards through the acid, D measuring the time taken for 10 cm 3 of gas to be collected
questions.text: When a piece of marble is added to hydrochloric acid, bubbles of carbon dioxide gas are given off. Which method is used to find the rate of the reaction? A counting the number of gas bubbles formed, B measuring the diameter of the gas bubbles, C measuring the speed at which the gas bubbles rise upwards through the acid, D measuring the time taken for 10 cm 3 of gas to be collected
options: A counting the number of gas bubbles formed
B measuring the diameter of the gas bubbles
C measuring the speed at which the gas bubbles rise upwards through the acid
D measuring the time taken for 10 cm 3 of gas to be collected

questions.question: Which statements about acids and bases are correct? 1   An acid reacts with a metal to give off hydrogen. 2   A base reacts with an ammonium salt to give off ammonia. 3   An acid reacts with a carbonate to give off carbon dioxide. 4   Alkaline solutions are orange in methyl orange. A 1, 2 and 3 B  1, 2 and 4 C  1, 3 and 4 D  2, 3 and 4
questions.text: Which statements about acids and bases are correct? 1   An acid reacts with a metal to give off hydrogen. 2   A base reacts with an ammonium salt to give off ammonia. 3   An acid reacts with a carbonate to give off carbon dioxide. 4   Alkaline solutions are orange in methyl orange. A 1, 2 and 3 B  1, 2 and 4 C  1, 3 and 4 D  2, 3 and 4
options: A 1, 2 and 3 B  1, 2 and 4 C  1, 3 and 4 D  2, 3 and 4

questions.question: Oxide 1 is a solid that reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid. Oxide 2 is a gas that reacts with sodium hydroxide solution. What are the formulae of the oxides? oxide 1    oxide 2 A CaO MgO, B MgO NO 2, C NO 2 SO 2, D SO 2 CaO
questions.text: Oxide 1 is a solid that reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid. Oxide 2 is a gas that reacts with sodium hydroxide solution. What are the formulae of the oxides? oxide 1    oxide 2 A CaO MgO, B MgO NO 2, C NO 2 SO 2, D SO 2 CaO

questions.question: In the preparation of zinc sulfate crystals, excess zinc oxide is added to dilute sulfuric acid. Why is an excess of zinc oxide added? A to make sure crystals are formed and not powder, B to avoid filtering the mixture, C to use up all of the sulfuric acid, D to use up all of the zinc oxide 7
questions.text: In the preparation of zinc sulfate crystals, excess zinc oxide is added to dilute sulfuric acid. Why is an excess of zinc oxide added? A to make sure crystals are formed and not powder, B to avoid filtering the mixture, C to use up all of the sulfuric acid, D to use up all of the zinc oxide 7
options: A to make sure crystals are formed and not powder
B to avoid filtering the mixture
C to use up all of the sulfuric acid
D to use up all of the zinc oxide 7

questions.question: Which statement about aqueous sodium hydroxide is correct? A When it is added to a solution containing sulfate ions, a white precipitate is formed., B When it is added to a solution of copper( II) ions, a blue precipitate is formed which dissolves in excess to give deep blue solution., C When it is added to a solution of iron( II) ions, a green precipitate is formed which does not dissolve in excess., D When it is added to ammonium chloride, a gas is produced which turns blue litmus red.
questions.text: Which statement about aqueous sodium hydroxide is correct? A When it is added to a solution containing sulfate ions, a white precipitate is formed., B When it is added to a solution of copper( II) ions, a blue precipitate is formed which dissolves in excess to give deep blue solution., C When it is added to a solution of iron( II) ions, a green precipitate is formed which does not dissolve in excess., D When it is added to ammonium chloride, a gas is produced which turns blue litmus red.
options: A When it is added to a solution containing sulfate ions, a white precipitate is formed.
B When it is added to a solution of copper( II) ions, a blue precipitate is formed which dissolves in excess to give deep blue solution.
C When it is added to a solution of iron( II) ions, a green precipitate is formed which does not dissolve in excess.
D When it is added to ammonium chloride, a gas is produced which turns blue litmus red.

questions.question: A period of the Periodic Table is shown. group    I   II   III   IV   V   VI   VII  VIII element   R   S   T   V   W   X   Y   Z The letters are not their chemical symbols. Which statement is correct? A Element R does not conduct electricity., B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound., C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule., D Element Z reacts with element T.
questions.text: A period of the Periodic Table is shown. group    I   II   III   IV   V   VI   VII  VIII element   R   S   T   V   W   X   Y   Z The letters are not their chemical symbols. Which statement is correct? A Element R does not conduct electricity., B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound., C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule., D Element Z reacts with element T.
options: A Element R does not conduct electricity.
B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound.
C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule.
D Element Z reacts with element T.

questions.question: Which statement about the elements in Group VII of the Periodic Table is correct? A Chlorine can displace bromine from bromides., B Group VII elements are all solids at room temperature., C Group VII elements occur as monoatomic covalent molecules., D Reactivity increases down Group VII.
questions.text: Which statement about the elements in Group VII of the Periodic Table is correct? A Chlorine can displace bromine from bromides., B Group VII elements are all solids at room temperature., C Group VII elements occur as monoatomic covalent molecules., D Reactivity increases down Group VII.
options: A Chlorine can displace bromine from bromides.
B Group VII elements are all solids at room temperature.
C Group VII elements occur as monoatomic covalent molecules.
D Reactivity increases down Group VII.

questions.question: The noble gases are in Group VIII of the Periodic Table. Which statement explains why noble gases are unreactive? A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells., B They all have full outer shells., C They are all gases., D They are all monoatomic.
questions.text: The noble gases are in Group VIII of the Periodic Table. Which statement explains why noble gases are unreactive? A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells., B They all have full outer shells., C They are all gases., D They are all monoatomic.
options: A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells.
B They all have full outer shells.

questions.question: Which statement is correct for all metals? A They conduct electricity when molten., B They gain electrons when they form ions., C They have a low density., D They have a low melting point.
questions.text: Which statement is correct for all metals? A They conduct electricity when molten., B They gain electrons when they form ions., C They have a low density., D They have a low melting point.
options: A They conduct electricity when molten.
B They gain electrons when they form ions.
C They have a low density.
D They have a low melting point.

questions.question: Which row describes the method of extraction of aluminium and iron from their ores? aluminium iron A electrolysis electrolysis, B electrolysis reduction with carbon, C reduction with carbon electrolysis, D reduction with carbon    reduction with carbon
questions.text: Which row describes the method of extraction of aluminium and iron from their ores? aluminium iron A electrolysis electrolysis, B electrolysis reduction with carbon, C reduction with carbon electrolysis, D reduction with carbon    reduction with carbon
options: D reduction with carbon    reduction with carbon

questions.question: Which statement about metals and their uses is correct? A Aluminium is used to make food containers because it is resistant to corrosion., B Aluminium is used to make aircraft wings because it is strong and has a high density., C Iron is used to make electrical wires because it is a good insulator of electricity., D Iron is used to make cooking utensils because it is easily recycled. 9
questions.text: Which statement about metals and their uses is correct? A Aluminium is used to make food containers because it is resistant to corrosion., B Aluminium is used to make aircraft wings because it is strong and has a high density., C Iron is used to make electrical wires because it is a good insulator of electricity., D Iron is used to make cooking utensils because it is easily recycled. 9
options: A Aluminium is used to make food containers because it is resistant to corrosion.
B Aluminium is used to make aircraft wings because it is strong and has a high density.
C Iron is used to make electrical wires because it is a good insulator of electricity.
D Iron is used to make cooking utensils because it is easily recycled. 9
